title: Timesheet Management with Two-Stage Approval Workflow + Published Schedule Visibility

requirements.summary: Add React Query hooks for timesheet CRUD operations
acceptanceCriteria: `useTimesheets` fetches data and returns loading/error/data states
`useSubmitTimesheet` resolves on #ok and rejects on #err
`useManagerReviewTimesheet` and `useHrReviewTimesheet` resolve on #ok and reject on #err
All mutations invalidate the timesheets query cache on success

file_operations.description: Add four new React Query hooks: `useTimesheets` (query hook that fetches all timesheets visible to the current user via actor.getTimesheets), `useSubmitTimesheet` (mutation hook calling actor.submitTimesheet, mapping ServiceResult_1 #ok to resolve and #err to reject, invalidating 'timesheets' query on success), `useManagerReviewTimesheet` (mutation hook calling actor.managerReviewTimesheet with id, approved boolean, and comment, mapping ServiceResult_1, invalidating 'timesheets' on success), and `useHrReviewTimesheet` (mutation hook calling actor.hrReviewTimesheet with id, approved boolean, and comment, mapping ServiceResult_1, invalidating 'timesheets' on success). Use the backend interface types Timesheet and TimesheetStatus from backend.d.ts.

requirements.summary: Create employee timesheet entry and submission page
acceptanceCriteria: Page renders a weekly timesheet entry grid with start/end time inputs per day
Hours auto-calculate per row and total is shown
Submit calls useSubmitTimesheet and shows a success toast
Validation blocks submission if any active row is missing start or end time
Previously submitted timesheets are listed with correct status badges
Page matches the existing dark navy/amber visual theme

file_operations.description: Create a new page component that displays a weekly timesheet entry form for employees. Include a week selector (defaulting to current week) using date utilities from lib/utils.ts. Render a table with seven rows (Monday–Sunday) showing date, start time input (type='time'), end time input (type='time'), and a read-only hours column that auto-calculates (endTime - startTime). Display a running total hours sum below the table. Add a 'Submit Timesheet' button that validates all filled rows have both start and end times, then calls useSubmitTimesheet with a Timesheet object (generate id via generateId, get employeeId from session context, map entries array, calculate totalHours, set status to 'submitted', set submittedAt to Date.now() * 1_000_000n). Show a toast on success using the Toaster from App.tsx. Below the form, render a list of the employee's previously submitted timesheets fetched via useTimesheets, displaying each with date range, total hours, and a StatusBadge component showing the current status (map TimesheetStatus enum values to readable labels: submitted='Pending Manager Review', managerApproved='Pending HR Review', managerDenied='Denied by Manager', hrApproved='Approved', hrDenied='Denied by HR'). Use the navy/amber color palette from tailwind.config.js and match the styling patterns in EmployeeDashboard.tsx.

requirements.summary: Create manager timesheet review page with two-stage approval
acceptanceCriteria: Pending timesheets (status #submitted) appear in the 'Pending Your Review' section
Approve action calls useManagerReviewTimesheet with approved=true
Deny action requires a comment and calls useManagerReviewTimesheet with approved=false
Success toast appears on approval or denial
Reviewed timesheets appear in the second section with status badges and comments
Page matches the existing dark navy/amber visual theme

file_operations.description: Create a new page component for managers to review team timesheets. Fetch all timesheets via useTimesheets and filter them: 'Pending Your Review' section shows timesheets with status='submitted', 'Reviewed / Processed' section shows all other statuses. For each pending timesheet, display employee username, week start date, total hours, and two action buttons: 'Approve' (calls useManagerReviewTimesheet with approved=true and an empty comment string) and 'Deny' (opens an inline modal or dialog requiring a denial comment input, then calls useManagerReviewTimesheet with approved=false and the comment). Show a toast notification on mutation success or error. For reviewed timesheets, display employee username, week date, total hours, current status badge using StatusBadge component, and managerComment if present. Use the navy/amber theme and follow the layout patterns from ManagerTimeOffPage.tsx for modal and action button styling.

requirements.summary: Create HR Admin final timesheet approval page
acceptanceCriteria: Manager-approved timesheets (status #managerApproved) appear in the 'Pending Final HR Approval' section
Final Approve calls useHrReviewTimesheet with approved=true
Final Deny requires an HR comment and calls useHrReviewTimesheet with approved=false
Success toast appears on final approval or denial
All Timesheets section shows all records with search and status filter
Page matches the existing dark navy/amber visual theme

file_operations.description: Create a new page component for HR Admin to perform final approval on timesheets. Fetch all timesheets via useTimesheets. Render two sections: 'Pending Final HR Approval' (filter timesheets where status='managerApproved', show employee username, week date, total hours, managerComment, and two action buttons 'Final Approve' and 'Final Deny'), and 'All Timesheets' (display all timesheet records with a search input filtering by employee name and a status dropdown filter). For 'Final Approve', call useHrReviewTimesheet with approved=true and an empty comment. For 'Final Deny', open an inline modal requiring an HR denial comment, then call useHrReviewTimesheet with approved=false and the comment. Show a toast on mutation success or error. In the All Timesheets section, render a table with columns for employee, week date, total hours, status badge (using StatusBadge component), manager comment, and HR comment. Use the navy/amber theme and follow the layout patterns from TimeOffRequestsPage.tsx for search/filter controls and table styling.

requirements.summary: Wire timesheet pages into role-based navigation and routing
acceptanceCriteria: HR Admin sidebar shows a 'Timesheets' link that navigates to /hr/timesheets and renders HRTimesheetPage
Manager sidebar shows a 'Timesheets' link that navigates to /manager/timesheets and renders ManagerTimesheetPage
Employee sidebar shows a 'Timesheet' link that navigates to /employee/timesheet and renders EmployeeTimesheetPage
All three routes are protected by their respective role guards

file_operations.description: Add a new navigation link in the sidebar menu array with label 'Timesheets', icon (use ClipboardList from lucide-react or similar), and path '/hr/timesheets'. Insert it after the 'Time Off Requests' link to maintain logical grouping.

Add a new navigation link in the sidebar menu array with label 'Timesheets', icon (use ClipboardList from lucide-react or similar), and path '/manager/timesheets'. Insert it after the 'Time Off' link to maintain logical grouping.

Add a new navigation link in the sidebar menu array with label 'Timesheet', icon (use ClipboardList from lucide-react or similar), and path '/employee/timesheet'. Insert it after the 'My Schedule' link to maintain logical grouping.

Register three new routes in the TanStack Router configuration: (1) In the HR Admin route tree, add a route with path '/hr/timesheets' that lazy-loads HRTimesheetPage and applies the hrAdminGuard. (2) In the Manager route tree, add a route with path '/manager/timesheets' that lazy-loads ManagerTimesheetPage and applies the managerGuard. (3) In the Employee route tree, add a route with path '/employee/timesheet' that lazy-loads EmployeeTimesheetPage and applies the employeeGuard. Follow the existing route registration patterns in the file.

requirements.summary: Ensure published shifts are immediately visible to employees without page reload
acceptanceCriteria: EmployeeSchedulePage.tsx fetches only published shifts (status #published)
The shifts query has staleTime: 0 or refetchInterval set so data is always fresh on mount
After a manager or HR Admin publishes a schedule, an employee navigating to their schedule page sees the new shifts
Draft shifts (status #draft) are NOT visible to employees on the schedule page or dashboard
EmployeeDashboard.tsx upcoming shifts section also only shows published shifts

file_operations.description: Modify the useShifts or equivalent query hook call to include query options with staleTime: 0 so the data is refetched on every component mount. Ensure the query filters shifts to only include those with status='published'. If the existing query does not filter by status, add client-side filtering in the component to exclude shifts where status is 'draft'. This ensures employees only see published shifts and the data is always fresh when they navigate to the page after a schedule publish action.

Modify the shifts query (or upcoming shifts logic) to include query options with staleTime: 0 and filter the fetched shifts to only display those with status='published'. Ensure draft shifts are excluded from the upcoming shifts section. This guarantees that the employee dashboard reflects only published schedules and is always up-to-date when the employee accesses it.
